query:
You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.50 (commit: e69ac9eb).

## Issue to Resolve

Indent IS_EXPRESSION and PREFIX_EXPRESSION
A multiline IS_EXPRESSION like below:
```
fun foo(any: Any) = any is
        Foo
```
is formatted by ktlint `0.49.x` to:
```
fun foo(any: Any) =
    any is
    Foo
```
while below was expected:
```
fun foo(any: Any) =
    any is
        Foo
```

Same for PREFIX_EXPRESSION:
```
fun foo(any: Int) = ++
    42
```

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

content.thinking:
Let me start by understanding the issue. The problem is about indentation of `IS_EXPRESSION` and `PREFIX_EXPRESSION` in Kotlin code. When a multiline `IS_EXPRESSION` or `PREFIX_EXPRESSION` is formatted, the continuation of the expression should be indented relative to the start of the expression, but it seems like ktlint is not doing this correctly.

Let me first look at the relevant files to understand the current implementation.

 460â†’    private fun visitLbrace(node: ASTNode) {

 492â†’    private fun ASTNode.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters() =
 493â†’        if (isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine()) {
 494â†’            // val fieldExample =
 495â†’            //      LongNameClass {
 496â†’            //              paramA,
 497â†’            //              paramB,
 498â†’            //              paramC ->
 499â†’            //          ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 500â†’            //      }
 501â†’            indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 502â†’        } else {
 503â†’            // Allow default IntelliJ IDEA formatting:
 504â†’            // val fieldExample =
 505â†’            //     LongNameClass { paramA,
 506â†’            //                     paramB,
 507â†’            //                     paramC ->
 508â†’            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 509â†’            //     }
 510â†’            // val fieldExample =
 511â†’            //     someFunction(
 512â†’            //         1,
 513â†’            //         2,
 514â†’            //     ) { paramA,
 515â†’            //         paramB,
 516â†’            //         paramC ->
 517â†’            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 518â†’            //     }
 519â†’            this
 520â†’                .takeIf { it.isPartOf(CALL_EXPRESSION) }
 521â†’                ?.treeParent
 522â†’                ?.leaves(false)
 523â†’                ?.takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
 524â†’                ?.sumOf { it.textLength }
 525â†’                ?.plus(2) // need to add spaces to compensate for "{ "
 526â†’                ?.let { length -> " ".repeat(length) }
 527â†’                ?: indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 528â†’        }

 544â†’    private fun visitValueParameter(node: ASTNode) {
 545â†’        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order

 547â†’        node
 548â†’            .findChildByType(EQ)
 549â†’            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 550â†’                nextToAstNode =
 551â†’                    startIndentContext(
 552â†’                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 553â†’                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 554â†’                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 555â†’            }
 556â†’
 557â†’        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 558â†’            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 559â†’            //     fun process(
 560â†’            //         aVariableWithAVeryLongName:
 561â†’            //             TypeWithAVeryLongNameThatDoesNotFitOnSameLineAsTheVariableName
 562â†’            //     ): List<Output>
 563â†’            node
 564â†’                .findChildByType(COLON)
 565â†’                ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 566â†’                    nextToAstNode =
 567â†’                        startIndentContext(
 568â†’                            fromAstNode = fromAstNode,

 570â†’                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 571â†’                }
 572â†’        }
 573â†’
 574â†’        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 575â†’        val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 576â†’        if (fromAstNode != node.firstChildNode &&
 577â†’            node.prevSibling { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == null &&
 578â†’            node == node.treeParent.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER)
 579â†’        ) {
 580â†’            nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 581â†’                fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 582â†’                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 583â†’            ).fromASTNode.prevLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpace() }!!
 584â†’        } else {
 585â†’            startIndentContext(
 586â†’                fromAstNode = node,
 587â†’                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,

 589â†’            )

 625â†’    private fun visitClass(node: ASTNode) {
 626â†’        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 627â†’        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 628â†’        node
 629â†’            .findChildByType(WHERE_KEYWORD)
 630â†’            ?.let { where ->
 631â†’                val typeConstraintList =
 632â†’                    requireNotNull(
 633â†’                        where.nextCodeSibling(),
 634â†’                    ) { "Can not find code sibling after WHERE in CLASS" }
 635â†’                require(
 636â†’                    typeConstraintList.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST,
 637â†’                ) { "Code sibling after WHERE in CLASS is not a TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST" }
 638â†’                nextToAstNode =
 639â†’                    startIndentContext(
 640â†’                        fromAstNode = where.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 641â†’                        toAstNode = typeConstraintList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 642â†’                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 643â†’            }
 644â†’
 645â†’        val primaryConstructor = node.findChildByType(PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
 646â†’        val containsConstructorKeyword = primaryConstructor?.findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_KEYWORD) != null
 647â†’        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && primaryConstructor != null && containsConstructorKeyword) {
 648â†’            // Contrary to the default IntelliJ IDEA formatter, ident both constructor and super type list as follows:
 649â†’            //     class Foo
 650â†’            //        @Bar1 @Bar2
 651â†’            //        constructor(
 652â†’            //            foo1: Foo1,
 653â†’            //            foo2: Foo2,
 654â†’            //        ) : Foobar1(
 655â†’            //                "foobar1",
 656â†’            //                "foobar2",
 657â†’            //            ),
 658â†’            //            FooBar2,
 659â†’            val superTypeList = node.findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 660â†’            nextToAstNode =
 661â†’                startIndentContext(
 662â†’                    fromAstNode = primaryConstructor.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 663â†’                    toAstNode =
 664â†’                        superTypeList
 665â†’                            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 666â†’                            ?: nextToAstNode,
 667â†’                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 668â†’
 669â†’            superTypeList
 670â†’                ?.findChildByType(COMMA)
 671â†’                ?.let { comma ->
 672â†’                    // In case of a multiline primary constructor the first super type is merged with the closing parenthesis of the
 673â†’                    // constructor. The start of the super type list does not activate the indent because it is not preceded by a newline.
 674â†’                    // To fix this, the super type entries on the next line (e.g. after the comma) have to be double indented.
 675â†’                    // Allow:
 676â†’                    //     class Foo(
 677â†’                    //         val bar1: Bar,
 678â†’                    //         val bar2: Bar,
 679â†’                    //     ) : FooBar(
 680â†’                    //             bar1,
 681â†’                    //             bar2
 682â†’                    //         ),
 683â†’                    //         BarFoo1
 684â†’                    val prevCodeLeaf =
 685â†’                        startIndentContext(
 686â†’                            fromAstNode = comma,
 687â†’                            toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 688â†’                            childIndent = indentConfig.indent.repeat(2),
 689â†’                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 690â†’                    startIndentContext(
 691â†’                        fromAstNode = primaryConstructor.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 692â†’                        toAstNode = prevCodeLeaf,
 693â†’                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 694â†’                }
 695â†’        } else {
 696â†’            node
 697â†’                .findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 698â†’                ?.let { superTypeList ->
 699â†’                    nextToAstNode =
 700â†’                        startIndentContext(
 701â†’                            fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 702â†’                            toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 703â†’                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 704â†’                }
 705â†’        }
 706â†’
 707â†’        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
 708â†’        startIndentContext(
 709â†’            fromAstNode = node,
 710â†’            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 711â†’            childIndent = "",

1138â†’    private fun ASTNode.normalizedIndent(emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit): String {
1139â†’        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1140â†’        return when (indentConfig.indentStyle) {
1141â†’            SPACE -> {

1143â†’                    emit(
1144â†’                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1145â†’                        "Unexpected tab character(s)",
1146â†’                        true,
1147â†’                    )
1148â†’                    indentConfig.toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndent)
1149â†’                } else {

1151â†’                }
1152â†’            }
1153â†’
1154â†’            TAB -> {

1160â†’                        "Unexpected space character(s)",
1161â†’                        true,
1162â†’                    )
1163â†’                    indentConfig

1165â†’                        .plus(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1166â†’                } else {

1168â†’                }

1358â†’                        //       """.trimIndent

1374â†’                                    it.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() ||
1375â†’                                    it.isClosingQuote()
1376â†’                            )
1377â†’                        ) {

1379â†’                                if (it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote()) {

1393â†’                                    true,
1394â†’                                )
1395â†’                                if (autoCorrect) {
1396â†’                                    (it.firstChildNode as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1397â†’                                        correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1398â†’                                    )

1415â†’                                            correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1416â†’                                        )
1417â†’                                    }
1418â†’                                }
1419â†’                            }

1421â†’                    }

 46â†’/**

 49â†’public class MultilineExpressionWrapping :
 50â†’    StandardRule(

 56â†’            ),

 61â†’
 62â†’    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 63â†’        indentConfig =
 64â†’            IndentConfig(

 67â†’            )

 71â†’        node: ASTNode,
 72â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,

 75â†’        if (node.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION &&
 76â†’            (node.treeParent.elementType !in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION || node.isRightHandSideOfBinaryExpression())

 78â†’            visitExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 79â†’        }
 80â†’        if (node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION &&
 81â†’            node.treeParent.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION
 82â†’        ) {
 83â†’            visitExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 84â†’        }
 85â†’    }

 89â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 90â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 91â†’    ) {
 92â†’        if (node.containsWhitespaceWithNewline() && node.needToWrapMultilineExpression()) {
 93â†’            node

 95â†’                .let { prevLeaf ->

 98â†’                        if (autoCorrect) {

100â†’                            node
101â†’                                .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
102â†’                                .nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() && !it.isPartOfComment() && it.elementType != COMMA }
103â†’                                ?.takeIf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
104â†’                                ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node))
105â†’                        }
106â†’                    }
107â†’                }
108â†’        }
109â†’    }

112â†’        val lastLeaf = lastChildLeafOrSelf()
113â†’        return firstChildLeafOrSelf()

115â†’            .takeWhile { it != lastLeaf }
116â†’            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || it.isRegularStringPartWithNewline() }

content.thinking:
Now let me understand the issue better. Looking at the issue description:
1. A multiline IS_EXPRESSION like `any is\n        Foo` should be formatted with the type indented relative to the `is` keyword.
2. Same for PREFIX_EXPRESSION like `++\n    42`.

I need to look at how the IndentationRule handles these cases. The issue is that when the expression is wrapped across lines, the right-hand side (type for IS_EXPRESSION, operand for PREFIX_EXPRESSION) needs to be indented.

Currently, looking at the code, I can see that IS_EXPRESSION and PREFIX_EXPRESSION are not handled in the IndentationRule. Let me see how BINARY_EXPRESSION and similar constructs are handled to understand the pattern.

The issue is that when we have:
```kotlin
fun foo(any: Any) = any is
        Foo
```

ktlint 0.49.x formats it to:
```kotlin
fun foo(any: Any) =
    any is
    Foo
```

But the expected is:
```kotlin
fun foo(any: Any) =
    any is
        Foo
```

So the problem is that `Foo` should be indented relative to the `is` expression.

Let me look at the structure of these expressions in Kotlin AST:
- IS_EXPRESSION: contains REFERENCE_EXPRESSION (left side), IS (keyword), TYPE_REFERENCE (right side)
- PREFIX_EXPRESSION: contains OPERATION_REFERENCE (++, --, etc.), followed by expression

The fix should add indentation context for IS_EXPRESSION and PREFIX_EXPRESSION similar to how BINARY_EXPRESSION is handled.

content.thinking:
Now I have a better understanding of how the IndentationRule works. Looking at the code, I can see:

1. For BINARY_EXPRESSION, the rule calls `visitBinaryExpression(node)` which handles the indentation
2. For IS_EXPRESSION and PREFIX_EXPRESSION, there's no explicit handling in the IndentationRule

Looking at the issue, the problem is that when we have:
```kotlin
fun foo(any: Any) =
    any is
    Foo
```

The expected is:
```kotlin
fun foo(any: Any) =
    any is
        Foo
```

So `Foo` should be indented relative to the IS expression.

Similarly for PREFIX_EXPRESSION:
```kotlin
fun foo(any: Int) = ++
    42
```

This is about adding indentation context for IS_EXPRESSION and PREFIX_EXPRESSION.

Looking at how similar constructs are handled:
- BINARY_EXPRESSION creates an indentation context
- BINARY_WITH_TYPE calls `startIndentContext(node)` 

The solution should be to add IS_EXPRESSION and PREFIX_EXPRESSION handling to the IndentationRule.

Let me add the handling for these:
1. IS_EXPRESSION - similar to BINARY_WITH_TYPE, just start an indent context
2. PREFIX_EXPRESSION - similar approach

Let me check the ElementType file to see if IS_EXPRESSION and PREFIX_EXPRESSION are already imported.

    @Nested
    inner class `Given a multiline IS_EXPRESSION` {
        @Test
        fun `Given a multiline IS_EXPRESSION in a function`() {
            val code =
                """
                fun foo(any: Any) =
                    any is
                        Foo
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
        }

        @Test
        fun `Given a multiline IS_EXPRESSION in a function with wrong indentation`() {
            val code =
                """
                fun foo(any: Any) =
                    any is
                    Foo
                """.trimIndent()
            val formattedCode =
                """
                fun foo(any: Any) =
                    any is
                        Foo
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code)
                .hasLintViolation(3, 1, "Unexpected indentation (4) (should be 8)")
                .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
        }

        @Test
        fun `Given a multiline negated IS_EXPRESSION in a function`() {
            val code =
                """
                fun foo(any: Any) =
                    any !is
                        Foo
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
        }
    }

    @Nested
    inner class `Given a multiline PREFIX_EXPRESSION` {
        @Test
        fun `Given a multiline PREFIX_EXPRESSION with increment operator`() {
            val code =
                """
                var foo = 1
                fun bar() = ++
                    foo
                """.trimIndent()
            val formattedCode =
                """
                var foo = 1
                fun bar() =
                    ++
                        foo
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code)
                .hasLintViolation(3, 1, "Unexpected indentation (4) (should be 8)")
                .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
        }

        @Test
        fun `Given a multiline PREFIX_EXPRESSION with decrement operator`() {
            val code =
                """
                var foo = 1
                fun bar() = --
                    foo
                """.trimIndent()
            val formattedCode =
                """
                var foo = 1
                fun bar() =
                    --
                        foo
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code)
                .hasLintViolation(3, 1, "Unexpected indentation (4) (should be 8)")
                .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
        }

        @Test
        fun `Given a multiline PREFIX_EXPRESSION with negation operator`() {
            val code =
                """
                fun bar(foo: Boolean) = !
                    foo
                """.trimIndent()
            val formattedCode =
                """
                fun bar(foo: Boolean) =
                    !
                        foo
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code)
                .hasLintViolation(2, 1, "Unexpected indentation (4) (should be 8)")
                .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
        }

        @Test
        fun `Given a multiline PREFIX_EXPRESSION with correct indentation`() {
            val code =
                """
                var foo = 1
                fun bar() =
                    ++
                        foo
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
        }
    }

1â†’# Changelog
  2â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3â†’This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4â†’
  5â†’## Unreleased
  6â†’
  7â†’### Added
  8â†’
  9â†’### Removed
 10â†’
 11â†’### Fixed
 12â†’
 13â†’* Allow to disable ktlint in `.editorconfig` for a glob ([#2100](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2100))
 14â†’* Fix wrapping of nested function literals `wrapping` ([#2106](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2106))
 15â†’* Do not indent class body for classes having a long super type list in code style `ktlint_official` as it is inconsistent compared to other class bodies `indent` [#2115](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2115)
 16â†’* In code style `ktlint_official` do not indent the closing parenthesis of a PARENTHESIZED expression `indent` [#920](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/920) 
 17â†’* Log message `Format was not able to resolve all violations which (theoretically) can be autocorrected in file ... in 3 consecutive runs of format` is now only displayed in case a new ktlint rule is actually needed. [#2129](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2129)
 18â†’* Fix wrapping of function signature in case the opening brace of the function body block exceeds the maximum line length. Fix upsert of whitespace into composite nodes. `function-signature` [#2130](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2130)
 19â†’* Fix spacing around colon in annotations `spacing-around-colon` ([#2093](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2093))
 20â†’
 21â†’### Changed
 22â†’
 23â†’* Update dependency gradle to v8.2.1 ([#2122](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2122))
 24â†’* Update dependency org.codehaus.janino:janino to v3.1.10  ([#2110](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2110))
 25â†’* Update dependency com.google.jimfs:jimfs to v1.3.0 ([#2112](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2112))
 26â†’
 27â†’## [0.50.0] - 2023-06-29
 28â†’
 29â†’### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
 30â†’
 31â†’The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
 32â†’
 33â†’API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
 34â†’
 35â†’The `internal:ktlint-suppression` rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` nor via `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations.
 36â†’
 37â†’### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
 38â†’
 39â†’In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
 40â†’
 41â†’In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
 42â†’
 43â†’Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
 44â†’
 45â†’* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
 46â†’* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
 47â†’* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
 48â†’
 49â†’Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
 50â†’
 51â†’In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
 52â†’
 53â†’At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
 54â†’
 55â†’At this point in time, it is not yet decided what the next steps will be. Ktlint might drop the support of the extension points entirely. Or, if the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is fully supported at the time that ktlint will be based on kotlin 1.9 it might be kept. In either case, the flag will be dropped in a next version of ktlint.
 56â†’
 57â†’### Added
 58â†’
 59â†’* Add new experimental rule `binary-expression-wrapping`. This rule wraps a binary expression in case the max line length is exceeded ([#1940](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1940))
 60â†’* Add flag to disable extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` to analyse impact on custom rules [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981)
 61â†’* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-file` for all code styles. A kotlin (script) file may not be empty ([#1074](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1074))
 62â†’* Add new experimental rule `statement-wrapping` which ensures function, class, or other blocks statement body doesn't start or end at starting or ending braces of the block ([#1938](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1938))
 63â†’* Add new experimental rule `blank-line-before-declaration`. This rule requires a blank line before class, function or property declarations ([#1939](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1939))
 64â†’* Wrap multiple statements on same line `wrapping` ([#1078](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1078))
 65â†’* Add new rule `ktlint-suppression` to replace the `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives with annotations. This rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` ([#1947](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1947))
 66â†’* Inform user about using `--format` option of KtLint CLI when finding a violation that can be autocorrected ([#1071](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1071))
 67â†’
 68â†’### Removed
 69â†’
 70â†’* Code which was deprecated in `0.49.x` is removed. Consult changelog of 0.49.x` released for more information. Summary of removed code: 
 71â†’
 72â†’### Fixed
 73â†’
 74â†’* Do not flag a (potential) mutable extension property in case the getter is annotated or prefixed with a modifier `property-naming` ([#2024](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2024))
 75â†’* Do not merge an annotated expression body with the function signature even if it fits on a single line ([#2043](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2043))
 76â†’* Ignore property with name `serialVersionUID` in `property-naming` ([#2045](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2045))
 77â†’* Prevent incorrect reporting of violations in case a nullable function type reference exceeds the maximum line length `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1324](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1324)) 
 78â†’* Prevent false negative on `else` branch when body contains only chained calls or binary expression ([#2057](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2057))
 79â†’* Fix indent when property value is wrapped to next line ([#2095](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2095)) 
 80â†’
 81â†’### Changed
 82â†’
 83â†’* Fix Java interoperability issues with `RuleId` and `RuleSetId` classes. Those classes were defined as value classes in `0.49.0` and `0.49.1`. Although the classes were marked with `@JvmInline` it seems that it is not possible to uses those classes from Java base API Consumers like Spotless. The classes have now been replaced with data classes ([#2041](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2041))
 84â†’* Update dependency `info.picocli:picocli` to v4.7.4
 85â†’* Update dependency `org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter` to v5.9.3
 86â†’* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.22` and Kotlin version to `1.8.22`.
 87â†’* Update dependency io.github.detekt.sarif4k:sarif4k to v0.4.0
 88â†’* Update dependency org.jetbrains.dokka:dokka-gradle-plugin to v1.8.20
 89â†’* Run format up to 3 times in case formatting introduces changes which also can be autocorrected ([#2084](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2084))
 90â†’
 91â†’## [0.49.1] - 2023-05-12
 92â†’
 93â†’### Added
 94â†’
 95â†’### Removed
 96â†’
 97â†’### Fixed
 98â†’* Store path of file containing a lint violation relative to the location of the baseline file itself ([#1962](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1962))
 99â†’* Print absolute path of file in lint violations when flag "--relative" is not specified in Ktlint CLI ([#1963](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1963)) 
100â†’* Handle parameter `--code-style=android_studio` in Ktlint CLI identical to deprecated parameter `--android` ([#1982](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1982))
101â†’* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if class without body is followed by multiple blank lines until end of file `no-consecutive-blank-lines` ([#1987](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1987))
102â†’* Allow to 'unset' the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` when using `ktlint_official` code style `function-signature` ([#1977](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1977))
103â†’* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if or operator at start of line is followed by dot qualified expression `indent` ([#1993](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1993))
104â†’* Fix indentation of multiline parameter list in function literal `indent` ([#1976](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1976))
105â†’* Restrict indentation of closing quotes to `ktlint_official` code style to keep formatting of other code styles consistent with `0.48.x` and before `indent` ([#1971](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1971))
106â†’* Extract rule `no-single-line-block-comment` from `comment-wrapping` rule. The `no-single-line-block-comment` rule is added as experimental rule to the `ktlint_official` code style, but it can be enabled explicitly for the other code styles as well. ([#1980](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1980))
107â†’* Clean-up unwanted logging dependencies ([#1998](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1998))
108â†’* Fix directory traversal for patterns referring to paths outside of current working directory or any of it child directories ([#2002](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2002))
109â†’* Prevent multiple expressions on same line separated by semicolon ([#1078](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1078))
110â†’
111â†’### Changed
112â†’
113â†’* Moved class `Baseline` from `ktlint-cli` to `ktlint-cli-reporter-baseline` so that Baseline functionality is reusable for API Consumers.
114â†’
115â†’## [0.49.0] - 2023-04-21
116â†’
117â†’WARNING: This version of KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgraded before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
118â†’
119â†’All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
120â†’* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
121â†’* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
122â†’* The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
123â†’* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
124â†’* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
125â†’
126â†’### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
127â†’
128â†’The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
129â†’
130â†’Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
131â†’* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
132â†’* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
133â†’* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
134â†’* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
135â†’
136â†’### Promote experimental rules to non-experimental
137â†’
138â†’The rules below have been promoted to non-experimental rules:
139â†’* [block-comment-initial-star-alignment](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#block-comment-initial-star-alignment)
140â†’* [class-naming](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#classobject-naming)

content.thinking:
Looking at the tests, I realize there's an issue with my tests. The test assumes that the `++` or `--` operator is on its own line after `=`, but that's not the actual use case in the issue. The issue shows:

```kotlin
fun foo(any: Any) = any is
        Foo
```

Being formatted to:
```kotlin
fun foo(any: Any) =
    any is
    Foo
```

But the expected is:
```kotlin
fun foo(any: Any) =
    any is
        Foo
```

So when the IS_EXPRESSION spans multiple lines (the type is on a new line), the type should be indented relative to the `is` keyword.

Let me re-read the issue to understand it better. The issue says that after wrapping (done by another rule), the continuation line (`Foo`) should be indented more than the `is` expression itself.

So my tests for IS_EXPRESSION look correct - when `any is` is followed by `Foo` on the next line with wrong indentation, it should be fixed.

For PREFIX_EXPRESSION, the test might be a bit off. Let me think about this...

Actually, looking at the PREFIX_EXPRESSION test, when we have:
```kotlin
fun bar() = ++
    foo
```

The `++foo` is the PREFIX_EXPRESSION. Within this expression, `foo` should be indented relative to `++`. So if the indent context is started on the PREFIX_EXPRESSION, then `foo` would get an additional indent.

But there's a subtle issue here. The tests I wrote assume that after the multiline expression wrapping rule runs, the PREFIX_EXPRESSION will be wrapped to a new line, and then the operand (`foo`) should be indented relative to the operator (`++`). The formattedCode in my tests shows:
```kotlin
fun bar() =
    ++
        foo
```

But this doesn't make practical sense. Let me reconsider the real use case.


I'm examining a specific scenario involving prefix expressions and their indentation. The key challenge is understanding how the operand should be positioned when the operator is on a separate line. The literal `42` presents an interesting edge case that requires careful consideration of formatting rules.

The core question revolves around maintaining consistent indentation when a prefix operator spans multiple lines. My previous assumptions about test cases might need refinement to accurately capture the intended formatting behavior.

The AST structure reveals a nested relationship between the prefix operation and its operand, which could significantly impact how indentation is applied. I'm particularly focused on how the formatting algorithm handles the positioning of the operand relative to the operator when they are on different lines.

The test case with `++foo` demonstrates a nuanced approach to indentation, suggesting that the operand might require additional indentation beyond the initial operator placement. This hints at a more complex formatting rule than initially anticipated.

The key observation is how the `startIndentContext` method potentially triggers an extra indentation level when encountering a prefix expression with a line break. This means the formatter might automatically adjust the indentation of subsequent elements based on the specific context of the prefix operation.

I need to carefully trace how the multiline-expression-wrapping rule interacts with this prefix expression formatting to understand the precise indentation mechanism at play.

The test case for `is` expression provides a clear example of how the indentation rule handles multi-line expressions, showing that the operand gets an additional indentation level when spanning multiple lines.

My next step is to design a test that specifically isolates the indentation behavior of prefix expressions, ensuring I capture the nuanced rules around line breaks and indentation contexts without interference from other formatting rules.

The key observation is that the PREFIX_EXPRESSION creates its own indentation context. When a prefix operator like `++` spans multiple lines, its operand should be indented relative to the original expression's base indent level. In this case, that means adding an extra indentation level to the child node.

I'll craft a test that demonstrates this precise indentation behavior, focusing on how the PREFIX_EXPRESSION rule handles multi-line scenarios independently of other formatting rules. The goal is to verify that the operand receives the correct indentation based on the prefix expression's context.

The test will need to show:
1. A prefix expression split across lines
2. Correct indentation of the operand
3. Verification that only the indentation rule is being applied

content.text: Looking at my tests, I realize there might be an issue with the PREFIX_EXPRESSION tests. The IndentationRule doesn't wrap expressions to new lines - that's done by the multiline-expression-wrapping rule. Let me fix the tests to properly test what the IndentationRule does:

{"file_path": "/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRuleTest.kt", "old_string": "    @Nested
    inner class `Given a multiline PREFIX_EXPRESSION` {
        @Test
        fun `Given a multiline PREFIX_EXPRESSION with increment operator`() {
            val code =
                """
                var foo = 1
                fun bar() = ++
                    foo
                """.trimIndent()
            val formattedCode =
                """
                var foo = 1
                fun bar() =
                    ++
                        foo
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code)
                .hasLintViolation(3, 1, "Unexpected indentation (4) (should be 8)")
                .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
        }

        @Test
        fun `Given a multiline PREFIX_EXPRESSION with decrement operator`() {
            val code =
                """
                var foo = 1
                fun bar() = --
                    foo
                """.trimIndent()
            val formattedCode =
                """
                var foo = 1
                fun bar() =
                    --
                        foo
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code)
                .hasLintViolation(3, 1, "Unexpected indentation (4) (should be 8)")
                .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
        }

        @Test
        fun `Given a multiline PREFIX_EXPRESSION with negation operator`() {
            val code =
                """
                fun bar(foo: Boolean) = !
                    foo
                """.trimIndent()
            val formattedCode =
                """
                fun bar(foo: Boolean) =
                    !
                        foo
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code)
                .hasLintViolation(2, 1, "Unexpected indentation (4) (should be 8)")
                .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
        }

        @Test
        fun `Given a multiline PREFIX_EXPRESSION with correct indentation`() {
            val code =
                """
                var foo = 1
                fun bar() =
                    ++
                        foo
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
        }
    }", "new_string": "    @Nested

        fun `Given a multiline PREFIX_EXPRESSION with increment operator and wrong indentation`() {
            val code =

                    foo

            val formattedCode =

                        foo
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code)
                .hasLintViolation(3, 1, "Unexpected indentation (4) (should be 8)")
                .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
        }

        @Test
        fun `Given a multiline PREFIX_EXPRESSION with decrement operator and wrong indentation`() {
            val code =
                """
                var foo = 1
                fun bar() = --
                    foo
                """.trimIndent()
            val formattedCode =
                """
                var foo = 1
                fun bar() = --
                        foo
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code)
                .hasLintViolation(3, 1, "Unexpected indentation (4) (should be 8)")
                .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
        }

        @Test
        fun `Given a multiline PREFIX_EXPRESSION with negation operator and wrong indentation`() {
            val code =
                """
                fun bar(foo: Boolean) = !
                    foo
                """.trimIndent()
            val formattedCode =
                """
                fun bar(foo: Boolean) = !
                        foo
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code)
                .hasLintViolation(2, 1, "Unexpected indentation (4) (should be 8)")
                .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
        }

        @Test
        fun `Given a multiline PREFIX_EXPRESSION with correct indentation`() {
            val code =
                """
                var foo = 1
                fun bar() =
                    ++
                        foo
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
        }
    }"}

content.thinking:
The tests look correct. Let me summarize what I have implemented:

## Summary of Changes

### Issue
Multiline `IS_EXPRESSION` and `PREFIX_EXPRESSION` were not being indented correctly. For example:
```kotlin
fun foo(any: Any) = any is
        Foo
```
Was being formatted to:
```kotlin
fun foo(any: Any) =
    any is
    Foo  // Wrong: Foo should be indented more
```

Instead of the expected:
```kotlin
fun foo(any: Any) =
    any is
        Foo  // Correct: Foo is indented relative to 'is'
```

### Fix
1. **IndentationRule.kt**: Added imports for `IS_EXPRESSION` and `PREFIX_EXPRESSION` element types, and added these to the list of element types that get an indent context via `startIndentContext(node)`. This ensures that children of these expressions are properly indented.

2. **IndentationRuleTest.kt**: Added comprehensive tests for both `IS_EXPRESSION` and `PREFIX_EXPRESSION` including:
   - Tests for correctly formatted code (no violations expected)
   - Tests for incorrectly formatted code (violations expected and corrected)
   - Tests for negated `is` expressions (`!is`)
   - Tests for different prefix operators (`++`, `--`, `!`)

3. **CHANGELOG.md**: Added an entry documenting this fix.

The fix is minimal and follows the same pattern used for similar expression types like `BINARY_WITH_TYPE`.

content.text:
The implementation is complete. Let me provide a summary of all the changes made to fix the issue:

## Summary of Changes

### Problem
Multiline `IS_EXPRESSION` (e.g., `any is Foo`) and `PREFIX_EXPRESSION` (e.g., `++foo`, `!foo`) were not being indented correctly when they spanned multiple lines. The continuation line was not being indented relative to the expression.

### Solution
Added handling for `IS_EXPRESSION` and `PREFIX_EXPRESSION` in the `IndentationRule` by calling `startIndentContext(node)` when these element types are encountered. This creates an indentation context that ensures child nodes (the type reference for `is` expressions, the operand for prefix expressions) are properly indented when they appear on a new line.

### Files Modified

1. **`ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`**:
   - Added imports for `IS_EXPRESSION` and `PREFIX_EXPRESSION`
   - Added these element types to the condition that calls `startIndentContext(node)` (alongside `BINARY_WITH_TYPE`, `SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY`, etc.)

2. **`ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRuleTest.kt`**:
   - Added `@Nested` test class `Given a multiline IS_EXPRESSION` with tests for:
     - Correctly formatted multiline IS_EXPRESSION
     - Incorrectly formatted multiline IS_EXPRESSION (expects lint violation and fix)
     - Negated IS_EXPRESSION (`!is`)
   - Added `@Nested` test class `Given a multiline PREFIX_EXPRESSION` with tests for:
     - Increment operator (`++`)
     - Decrement operator (`--`)
     - Negation operator (`!`)
     - Correctly formatted multiline PREFIX_EXPRESSION

3. **`CHANGELOG.md`**:
   - Added entry: "Indent `IS_EXPRESSION` and `PREFIX_EXPRESSION` when the expression is multiline `indent`"

### Example of Fixed Behavior

**IS_EXPRESSION:**
```kotlin
// Before (wrong):
fun foo(any: Any) =
    any is
    Foo

// After (correct):
fun foo(any: Any) =
    any is
        Foo
```

**PREFIX_EXPRESSION:**
```kotlin
// Before (wrong):
fun bar() = ++
    foo

// After (correct):
fun bar() = ++
        foo
```

    @Nested
    inner class `Given a multiline IS_EXPRESSION` {
        @Test
        fun `Given a multiline IS_EXPRESSION in a function`() {
            val code =
                """
                fun foo(any: Any) =
                    any is
                        Foo
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
        }

        @Test
        fun `Given a multiline IS_EXPRESSION in a function with wrong indentation`() {
            val code =
                """
                fun foo(any: Any) =
                    any is
                    Foo
                """.trimIndent()
            val formattedCode =
                """
                fun foo(any: Any) =
                    any is
                        Foo
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code)
                .hasLintViolation(3, 1, "Unexpected indentation (4) (should be 8)")
                .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
        }

        @Test
        fun `Given a multiline negated IS_EXPRESSION in a function`() {
            val code =
                """
                fun foo(any: Any) =
                    any !is
                        Foo
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
        }
    }

    @Nested
    inner class `Given a multiline PREFIX_EXPRESSION` {
        @Test
        fun `Given a multiline PREFIX_EXPRESSION with increment operator`() {
            val code =
                """
                var foo = 1
                fun bar() = ++
                    foo
                """.trimIndent()
            val formattedCode =
                """
                var foo = 1
                fun bar() =
                    ++
                        foo
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code)
                .hasLintViolation(3, 1, "Unexpected indentation (4) (should be 8)")
                .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
        }

        @Test
        fun `Given a multiline PREFIX_EXPRESSION with decrement operator`() {
            val code =
                """
                var foo = 1
                fun bar() = --
                    foo
                """.trimIndent()
            val formattedCode =
                """
                var foo = 1
                fun bar() =
                    --
                        foo
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code)
                .hasLintViolation(3, 1, "Unexpected indentation (4) (should be 8)")
                .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
        }

        @Test
        fun `Given a multiline PREFIX_EXPRESSION with negation operator`() {
            val code =
                """
                fun bar(foo: Boolean) = !
                    foo
                """.trimIndent()
            val formattedCode =
                """
                fun bar(foo: Boolean) =
                    !
                        foo
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code)
                .hasLintViolation(2, 1, "Unexpected indentation (4) (should be 8)")
                .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
        }

        @Test
        fun `Given a multiline PREFIX_EXPRESSION with correct indentation`() {
            val code =
                """
                var foo = 1
                fun bar() =
                    ++
                        foo
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
        }
    }

1â†’# Changelog
  2â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3â†’This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4â†’
  5â†’## Unreleased
  6â†’
  7â†’### Added
  8â†’
  9â†’### Removed
 10â†’
 11â†’### Fixed
 12â†’
 13â†’* Allow to disable ktlint in `.editorconfig` for a glob ([#2100](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2100))
 14â†’* Fix wrapping of nested function literals `wrapping` ([#2106](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2106))
 15â†’* Do not indent class body for classes having a long super type list in code style `ktlint_official` as it is inconsistent compared to other class bodies `indent` [#2115](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2115)
 16â†’* In code style `ktlint_official` do not indent the closing parenthesis of a PARENTHESIZED expression `indent` [#920](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/920) 
 17â†’* Log message `Format was not able to resolve all violations which (theoretically) can be autocorrected in file ... in 3 consecutive runs of format` is now only displayed in case a new ktlint rule is actually needed. [#2129](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2129)
 18â†’* Fix wrapping of function signature in case the opening brace of the function body block exceeds the maximum line length. Fix upsert of whitespace into composite nodes. `function-signature` [#2130](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2130)
 19â†’* Fix spacing around colon in annotations `spacing-around-colon` ([#2093](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2093))
 20â†’
 21â†’### Changed
 22â†’
 23â†’* Update dependency gradle to v8.2.1 ([#2122](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2122))
 24â†’* Update dependency org.codehaus.janino:janino to v3.1.10  ([#2110](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2110))
 25â†’* Update dependency com.google.jimfs:jimfs to v1.3.0 ([#2112](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2112))
 26â†’
 27â†’## [0.50.0] - 2023-06-29
 28â†’
 29â†’### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
 30â†’
 31â†’The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
 32â†’
 33â†’API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
 34â†’
 35â†’The `internal:ktlint-suppression` rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` nor via `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations.
 36â†’
 37â†’### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
 38â†’
 39â†’In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
 40â†’
 41â†’In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
 42â†’
 43â†’Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
 44â†’
 45â†’* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
 46â†’* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
 47â†’* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
 48â†’
 49â†’Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
 50â†’
 51â†’In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
 52â†’
 53â†’At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
 54â†’
 55â†’At this point in time, it is not yet decided what the next steps will be. Ktlint might drop the support of the extension points entirely. Or, if the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is fully supported at the time that ktlint will be based on kotlin 1.9 it might be kept. In either case, the flag will be dropped in a next version of ktlint.
 56â†’
 57â†’### Added
 58â†’
 59â†’* Add new experimental rule `binary-expression-wrapping`. This rule wraps a binary expression in case the max line length is exceeded ([#1940](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1940))
 60â†’* Add flag to disable extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` to analyse impact on custom rules [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981)
 61â†’* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-file` for all code styles. A kotlin (script) file may not be empty ([#1074](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1074))
 62â†’* Add new experimental rule `statement-wrapping` which ensures function, class, or other blocks statement body doesn't start or end at starting or ending braces of the block ([#1938](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1938))
 63â†’* Add new experimental rule `blank-line-before-declaration`. This rule requires a blank line before class, function or property declarations ([#1939](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1939))
 64â†’* Wrap multiple statements on same line `wrapping` ([#1078](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1078))
 65â†’* Add new rule `ktlint-suppression` to replace the `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives with annotations. This rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` ([#1947](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1947))
 66â†’* Inform user about using `--format` option of KtLint CLI when finding a violation that can be autocorrected ([#1071](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1071))
 67â†’
 68â†’### Removed
 69â†’
 70â†’* Code which was deprecated in `0.49.x` is removed. Consult changelog of 0.49.x` released for more information. Summary of removed code: 
 71â†’
 72â†’### Fixed
 73â†’
 74â†’* Do not flag a (potential) mutable extension property in case the getter is annotated or prefixed with a modifier `property-naming` ([#2024](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2024))
 75â†’* Do not merge an annotated expression body with the function signature even if it fits on a single line ([#2043](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2043))
 76â†’* Ignore property with name `serialVersionUID` in `property-naming` ([#2045](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2045))
 77â†’* Prevent incorrect reporting of violations in case a nullable function type reference exceeds the maximum line length `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1324](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1324)) 
 78â†’* Prevent false negative on `else` branch when body contains only chained calls or binary expression ([#2057](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2057))
 79â†’* Fix indent when property value is wrapped to next line ([#2095](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2095)) 
 80â†’
 81â†’### Changed
 82â†’
 83â†’* Fix Java interoperability issues with `RuleId` and `RuleSetId` classes. Those classes were defined as value classes in `0.49.0` and `0.49.1`. Although the classes were marked with `@JvmInline` it seems that it is not possible to uses those classes from Java base API Consumers like Spotless. The classes have now been replaced with data classes ([#2041](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2041))
 84â†’* Update dependency `info.picocli:picocli` to v4.7.4
 85â†’* Update dependency `org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter` to v5.9.3
 86â†’* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.22` and Kotlin version to `1.8.22`.
 87â†’* Update dependency io.github.detekt.sarif4k:sarif4k to v0.4.0
 88â†’* Update dependency org.jetbrains.dokka:dokka-gradle-plugin to v1.8.20
 89â†’* Run format up to 3 times in case formatting introduces changes which also can be autocorrected ([#2084](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2084))
 90â†’
 91â†’## [0.49.1] - 2023-05-12
 92â†’
 93â†’### Added
 94â†’
 95â†’### Removed
 96â†’
 97â†’### Fixed
 98â†’* Store path of file containing a lint violation relative to the location of the baseline file itself ([#1962](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1962))
 99â†’* Print absolute path of file in lint violations when flag "--relative" is not specified in Ktlint CLI ([#1963](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1963)) 
100â†’* Handle parameter `--code-style=android_studio` in Ktlint CLI identical to deprecated parameter `--android` ([#1982](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1982))
101â†’* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if class without body is followed by multiple blank lines until end of file `no-consecutive-blank-lines` ([#1987](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1987))
102â†’* Allow to 'unset' the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` when using `ktlint_official` code style `function-signature` ([#1977](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1977))
103â†’* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if or operator at start of line is followed by dot qualified expression `indent` ([#1993](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1993))
104â†’* Fix indentation of multiline parameter list in function literal `indent` ([#1976](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1976))
105â†’* Restrict indentation of closing quotes to `ktlint_official` code style to keep formatting of other code styles consistent with `0.48.x` and before `indent` ([#1971](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1971))
106â†’* Extract rule `no-single-line-block-comment` from `comment-wrapping` rule. The `no-single-line-block-comment` rule is added as experimental rule to the `ktlint_official` code style, but it can be enabled explicitly for the other code styles as well. ([#1980](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1980))
107â†’* Clean-up unwanted logging dependencies ([#1998](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1998))
108â†’* Fix directory traversal for patterns referring to paths outside of current working directory or any of it child directories ([#2002](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2002))
109â†’* Prevent multiple expressions on same line separated by semicolon ([#1078](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1078))
110â†’
111â†’### Changed
112â†’
113â†’* Moved class `Baseline` from `ktlint-cli` to `ktlint-cli-reporter-baseline` so that Baseline functionality is reusable for API Consumers.
114â†’
115â†’## [0.49.0] - 2023-04-21
116â†’
117â†’WARNING: This version of KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgraded before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
118â†’
119â†’All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
120â†’* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
121â†’* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
122â†’* The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
123â†’* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
124â†’* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
125â†’
126â†’### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
127â†’
128â†’The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
129â†’
130â†’Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
131â†’* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
132â†’* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
133â†’* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
134â†’* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
135â†’
136â†’### Promote experimental rules to non-experimental
137â†’
138â†’The rules below have been promoted to non-experimental rules:
139â†’* [block-comment-initial-star-alignment](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#block-comment-initial-star-alignment)
140â†’* [class-naming](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#classobject-naming)
141â†’* [comment-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#comment-wrapping)
142â†’* [function-return-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-return-type-spacing)
143â†’* [function-start-of-body-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-start-of-body-spacing)
144â†’* [function-type-reference-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-type-reference-spacing)
145â†’* [fun-keyword-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#fun-keyword-spacing)
146â†’* [kdoc-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#kdoc-wrapping)
147â†’* [modifier-list-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#modifier-list-spacing)
148â†’* [nullable-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#nullable-type-spacing)
149â†’* [spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis)
150â†’* [unnecessary-parentheses-before-trailing-lambda](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#unnecessary-parenthesis-before-trailing-lambda)

                    foo

            val formattedCode =

            indentationRuleAssertThat(code)

            val code =

                fun bar() = --
                    foo

            val formattedCode =

                fun bar() =
                    --
                        foo
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code)

1â†’# Changelog
 2â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 3â†’This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 4â†’
 5â†’## Unreleased
 6â†’
 7â†’### Added
 8â†’
 9â†’### Removed
10â†’
11â†’### Fixed
12â†’
13â†’* Indent `IS_EXPRESSION` and `PREFIX_EXPRESSION` when the expression is multiline `indent`
14â†’* Allow to disable ktlint in `.editorconfig` for a glob ([#2100](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2100))
15â†’* Fix wrapping of nested function literals `wrapping` ([#2106](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2106))
16â†’* Do not indent class body for classes having a long super type list in code style `ktlint_official` as it is inconsistent compared to other class bodies `indent` [#2115](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2115)
17â†’* In code style `ktlint_official` do not indent the closing parenthesis of a PARENTHESIZED expression `indent` [#920](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/920) 
18â†’* Log message `Format was not able to resolve all violations which (theoretically) can be autocorrected in file ... in 3 consecutive runs of format` is now only displayed in case a new ktlint rule is actually needed. [#2129](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2129)
19â†’* Fix wrapping of function signature in case the opening brace of the function body block exceeds the maximum line length. Fix upsert of whitespace into composite nodes. `function-signature` [#2130](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2130)
20â†’* Fix spacing around colon in annotations `spacing-around-colon` ([#2093](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2093))
21â†’
22â†’### Changed
23â†’
24â†’* Update dependency gradle to v8.2.1 ([#2122](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2122))
25â†’* Update dependency org.codehaus.janino:janino to v3.1.10  ([#2110](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2110))
